title: PM Lane 169 - Local PM Operating Queue Grouping
authority: repo technical authority; PM lane implementation authority; final integration authority
objective: Reduce Project Miner Temp Power operating-queue scan burden by grouping the existing Local PM Operating Queue cards without changing queue content, exports, storage behavior, or write boundaries.
scope: Grouped the existing Local PM Operating Queue into Local Review Moves, Approval Submission Boundary, and Future Import Boundary.
Preserved the same seven browser-local queue cards, labels, dynamic detail text, status pills, complete/next/blocked summary count, and no-write language.
Kept the operating-queue disclosure behavior local and stateless with no operating-queue localStorage key.
Used a read-only sidecar to scout the next safe follow-on tranche while VS Code Codex retained PM implementation and final integration authority.
guardrails: No Supabase, Render, Vercel, or Olares product action.
No approval POST or approval-row creation.

No field authorization, assignment, schedule/status, durable field record, production tracking, customer report, completion evidence, billing, payroll, invoice, accounting, or external finance-system write.
No new export action, export handler, filename, payload version, localStorage key, backend route, service/auth/ingress change, or autonomous AI business-state mutation.

validation_result: PASS - operations-web typecheck passed; operations-web build passed; focused Playwright pm import-intake smoke passed; packet JSON parsed; PM Lane 169 guardrail rg passed; git diff --check passed with only expected CRLF warnings on touched and unrelated markdown files.
